perturbation.original_text: 5.2 In addition, ebix agrees to pay to About a fee of [**] for [**] and [**] from [**] up to [**] (the "Application Fees"). The payment of the Application Fees are to be due within 30 days after the end of the month in which the Application Fees were generated. Such payments shall be accompanied by a report stating the number of Filled Application Forms generated during the previous month, as well as the amount due to About. Ebix will track the number of Filled Application Forms generated by About Customers.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 In addition, ebix agrees to pay to About a fee of [**] for [**] and [**] from [**] up to [**] (the "Application Fees"). The payment of the Application Fees are to be due within 15 days after the end of the month in which the Application Fees were generated. Such payments shall be accompanied by a report stating the number of Filled Application Forms generated during the previous month, as well as the amount due to About. Ebix will track the number of Filled Application Forms generated by About Customers.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadline for Application Fees from 30 days to 15 days after the end of the month. This conflicts with Section 5.3 which stipulates interest accrual starting 30 days after the end of the month, creating ambiguity regarding the actual due date and when interest charges begin to apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 If any of the payments are not received on or before the appropriate dates as described above, About shall have the right to charge ebix interest on the overdue amount at the rate of 6% per year,, calculated from 30 days after the end of the month for which payment is made until the date of ebix's payment of such amount, which interest shall be in addition to such fees due and owing About. In addition, About shall be entitled to its reasonable costs and expenses (including attorneys' fees) in connection with any action to collect fees under or to otherwise enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline for Application Fees from 30 days to 15 days after the end of the month, directly contradicting Section 5.3 which calculates interest starting 30 days after the end of the month. This creates a clear inconsistency as to when the payment is considered overdue and when interest charges begin to apply, thereby triggering the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type. The change directly breaks the compatibility between the two sections.

perturbation.original_text: 13.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement if the other (i) fails to make any payment required to be made by it hereunder for more than twenty (20) business days after such party shall have received notice from the other party of the failure of payment thereof, or (ii) commits a material breach of this Agreement that is not cured within thirty (30) calendar days after receipt of written notice of the breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement if the other (i) fails to make any payment required to be made by it hereunder for more than ten (10) business days after such party shall have received notice from the other party of the failure of payment thereof, or (ii) commits a material breach of this Agreement that is not cured within thirty (30) calendar days after receipt of written notice of the breach.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies the period allowed to cure payment failures before termination can occur, by shortening it from twenty (20) business days to ten (10) business days. This creates an in-text contradiction in relation to section 5.3. The section 5.3 specifies a 30-day period before interest applies, however, section 13.2 gives About the option to terminate the contract, should eBix default in 10 days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 If any of the payments are not received on or before the appropriate dates as described above, About shall have the right to charge ebix interest on the overdue amount at the rate of 6% per year,, calculated from 30 days after the end of the month for which payment is made until the date of ebix's payment of such amount, which interest shall be in addition to such fees due and owing About. In addition, About shall be entitled to its reasonable costs and expenses (including attorneys' fees) in connection with any action to collect fees under or to otherwise enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the cure period for payment failures from 20 business days to 10 business days. Section 5.3 states that interest will be charged on overdue payments only after 30 days from the end of the month in which the payment was due. Therefore, Section 13.2, as amended, now allows About to terminate the contract before interest even begins to accrue under Section 5.3, creating a direct contradiction. This creates a strong contradiction as it alters the timing of enforcement actions, allowing termination before the original terms for interest charges are triggered, and falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
